commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Crabpot-specific HTTP-route synthetic response helpers, classifies two memory-lancedb blocked probe reasons in dashboard CI policy, and adds focused regression tests for both paths.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the current pinned inspector accepts registrationProbeInputs but its default HTTP-route response lacks setHeader, matching the failing handler shape covered by the new regression test.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer review should decide the CI-policy allowlist and draft readiness; there is no narrow mechanical repair for ClawSweeper to make.

target.title: fix(dashboard): cover beta fixture probe gaps
target.body:
## Summary
- add Crabpot HTTP-route synthetic response helpers so Prometheus-style route handlers can execute instead of failing on missing `setHeader`
- classify the current memory-lancedb synthetic blockers as expected harness/runtime gaps in the dashboard CI policy
- add focused regression tests for both paths

## Verification
- `node --test test/synthetic-probes.test.mjs test/ci-policy.test.mjs`
- `CRABPOT_EXECUTE_ISOLATED=1 node scripts/execute-workspace-plan.mjs --fixture diagnostics-prometheus --openclaw /Users/m1/GIT/_Perso/openclaw --continue-on-error`
- `git diff --check`

## Notes
- OpenClaw main plugin prerelease is green; the active Crabpot failure is the dashboard policy/probe path.
- Local memory-lancedb execution on this machine hits Node 26 strip-only TypeScript syntax before synthetic probes; the failing dashboard lane runs Node 22.
target.author: vincentkoc
